title: Fix Select crash and update Aaron artwork without breaking comic viewer

requirements.summary: Remove all empty-string SelectItem values by introducing a non-empty sentinel while preserving the “Default” voice behavior in narration controls.
acceptanceCriteria: App no longer crashes on load with the Radix error: "A <Select.Item /> must have a value prop that is not an empty string".
In `frontend/src/components/StoryNarrationControl.tsx`, the three voice dropdowns no longer render any `<SelectItem value="">…</SelectItem>` option.
Choosing the UI option that represents the default voice still results in using the default voice behavior (i.e., the app does not force a specific selected voice).

file_operations.description: Replace the three empty-string SelectItem values with a non-empty sentinel value and map sentinel selection back to the empty internal state so “Default” continues to mean “no explicit voice selected”. Use the shadcn-ui Select component as currently composed (do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add guardrails to ensure comic panels and the characters gallery still render after the Select crash fix, without altering existing story data.
acceptanceCriteria: The `ComicViewer` still renders comic panels (not a blank/black screen) and remains usable after the Select crash fix.
The `CharactersGallery` still renders all existing characters, including Aaron, after the Select crash fix.
No changes remove or significantly alter the existing chapter/panel data in `frontend/src/lib/comicData.ts` beyond what is required for stability fixes.

file_operations.description: Add a safe empty-state rendering path (e.g., a visible alert/message) when there are zero panels to display so the viewer never appears as a blank/black screen due to unexpected upstream state; keep existing panel/chapter data usage intact.

Add a small non-destructive render guard (e.g., ensure the gallery renders even if a character is missing an asset/alt text) to prevent regressions while keeping existing character list behavior unchanged.

requirements.summary: Update Aaron’s portrait asset to depict dark skin and short/visible hair and keep the gallery wired to the updated image.
acceptanceCriteria: The Characters Gallery displays Aaron with dark skin and short/visible hair in the character tile artwork.
Aaron’s character entry in `frontend/src/lib/characters.ts` references the updated artwork filename (or the original filename if overwritten) and remains correctly displayed.
All user-facing text remains in English for this change (e.g., alt text updates remain English).

file_operations.description: Add the updated Aaron character portrait asset (dark skin + short/visible hair in bold comic/halftone style) as a static frontend asset at this exact path.
Update Aaron’s `artworkSrc` to reference `/assets/generated/character-aaron-v2.dim_768x768.png` and ensure Aaron’s `artworkAlt` remains English while reflecting the updated appearance.
